title: centaurea virgata var. squarrosa
intro: Centaurea virgata var. squarrosa, also known as a type of knapweed, is a perennial plant species in the daisy family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to have health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving rheumatic pain, due to its salicylic acid content. Centaurea virgata var. squarrosa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
